Politics.headline: Trump is dismantling democracy, reports find. And, Treasury to take over student loans
Politics.detail: An annual V-Dem report dropped the U.S.'s democracy ranking from 20 to 51 among 179 countries, while a Freedom House report noted significant declines in political rights and civil liberties in the U.S. last year; also, the Treasury is set to take over student loans.

Politics.headline: Politics Friday: Consumer safeguards gain bipartisan support at the Capitol
Politics.detail: In the 2026 legislative session, consumer protection measures are receiving bipartisan support, addressing issues like prices, scams, and available recourses for those who have been defrauded.

Politics.headline: Labour claims extremist candidate revelations show Reform UK's launch in Scotland has fallen apart – UK politics live
Politics.detail: Labour asserts that the exposure of extremist views among some of Reform UK's Holyrood candidates, who have been accused of spreading misinformation and discriminatory remarks, indicates a collapse of the party's launch in Scotland.

Politics.headline: Trump aides tried to block appointment of Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to US, reports say – US politics live
Politics.detail: Donald Trump's team reportedly attempted to prevent Keir Starmer from appointing Peter Mandelson as the UK ambassador to the US, with concerns raised about Mandelson's past disparaging remarks about Trump and his perceived arrogance; Mandelson was later dismissed due to connections with Jeffrey Epstein.

Politics.headline: Department of Justice says Kansas handed over private voter data
Politics.detail: The U.S. Department of Justice claims that Kansas provided the federal government with private voter data, including Social Security numbers and DMV registration information, a claim disputed by the Kansas Secretary of State's office.

Finance & Economy.headline: US equity futures fall as energy prices stoke stagflation fears
Finance & Economy.detail: US equity futures fell to their lowest since November as high energy prices continued to drive stagflation fears, with the March contracts for the three main indices around 0.5% lower. Ongoing strikes across the Middle East and the near-complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz continued to disrupt regional energy supplies.

Finance & Economy.headline: China's FDI Falls 5.7% in Early 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows into China declined 5.7% year-over-year in January-February 2026, with the manufacturing sector securing CNY 47.52 billion and services accounting for CNY 111.22 billion of the total. High-tech industries saw FDI surge 20.4% to CNY 63.21 billion, representing 39.2% of total inflows.

Finance & Economy.headline: Treasury Yields Climb, 10-Year at Highest Since 2025
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices reversed earlier declines to climb back to levels last seen in 2022, underscoring persistent volatility in energy markets amidst strikes in the Middle East. The Federal Reserve kept the federal funds rate unchanged, but updated projections still point to one rate cut this year, highlighting uncertainty about the economic impact of the war and elevated upside risks to inflation.

Finance & Economy.headline: Household energy bills in Great Britain 'could rise to almost £2,000 a year' amid Iran war shock
Finance & Economy.detail: Household energy bills in Great Britain could rise significantly amid the shock of the Iran war. The ongoing conflict has led to concerns about energy supplies and potential price increases for consumers.

Finance & Economy.headline: Small businesses reeling from tariffs brace for spiking oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Small businesses are experiencing anxiety due to tariffs and are now bracing for spiking oil prices because of the war in Iran. The increased cost of oil is expected to further strain small business finances.

Technology.headline: ZTE HI-OTN Achievements Earn Dual Top-Scored Paper Recognition at OFC 2026
Technology.detail: ZTE Corporation was recognized for breakthroughs in a real-time 1.2 Tb/s S-band silicon photonics transceiver and long-haul multi-AIDC LLM training field trials at the Optical Fiber Communication Conference and Exhibition (OFC) 2026. These advancements reinforce ZTE's HI-OTN solution for ultra-wide spectrum transmission and distributed intelligent computing.

Technology.headline: Safran's ENGINeUS electric motor awarded at the 2026 Aviation Week Laureates
Technology.detail: Safran Electrical & Power's ENGINeUS electric motor won in the Commercial Aviation category at the 68th Aviation Week Laureates Awards. The ENGINeUS motor is recognized as a leading innovation in aviation technology and was the world's first electric motor for hybrid and all-electric aircraft to achieve certification from the European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) in 2025.

Technology.headline: Weekly Intelligence Report – 20 March 2026
Technology.detail: CYFIRMA's assessment indicates that Zollo is a ransomware threat that encrypts data on compromised systems, leading to restricted access to critical files and disruption of normal operations. This week's report also focuses on “Raton RAT,” a remote access tool designed to provide an external operator with control over a compromised Windows system.

Technology.headline: Tech News Roundup - 20.03.26
Technology.detail: This week's South West Tech News roundup includes Triggerfish Animation Studios choosing Bristol as its UK base and Bristol garage tech provider Motasoft being acquired by ClearCourse. A new Strategic Economic Audit highlights the West of England as one of the UK’s strongest regional economies, but points to challenges around housing, transport, and skills.

Technology.headline: Cyber Chaos This Week: iPhones Under Attack, Android VPN Glitches, and the Free Parking Hack You Won't Believe
Technology.detail: This week's biggest cybersecurity stories affect everyone, including what you pay for parking. Google is imposing a 24-hour safety wait to activate Android App Sideloading to stop Android malware and scams.

Science.headline: Huge Study Finds No Evidence Cannabis Helps Anxiety, Depression, or PTSD
Science.detail: The largest review of medicinal cannabis to date found it doesn't effectively treat anxiety, depression, or PTSD, and researchers warn it could even make mental health worse, raising risks like psychosis.

Science.headline: Shingles Vaccine Might Double as Powerful Heart Protector
Science.detail: A shingles vaccine might double as a powerful heart protector; in people already at high risk, it cut major cardiac events by 46% and deaths by an impressive 66% within a year. Scientists think preventing shingles may also stop clot-related issues.

Science.headline: Scientists Solve 12,800-year-old Climate Mystery Hidden in Greenland Ice
Science.detail: Scientists have solved a 12,800-year-old climate mystery hidden in Greenland ice.

Science.headline: H5N1 in marine mammals is spreading: Research tallies over 50,000 seals and sea lions killed along South America's coast
Science.detail: Research indicates that the H5N1 virus is spreading amongst marine mammals, with a death toll of over 50,000 seals and sea lions along the coast of South America.

Science.headline: NASA returns moon rocket to pad and targets 1 April launch
Science.detail: NASA is targeting an April 1 launch after returning the moon rocket to the pad.

Health.headline: Type 1 diabetes linked to significantly higher dementia risk, large U.S. study finds
Health.detail: A new study suggests that the risk of dementia is higher in people with type 1 diabetes compared to those without, reinforcing the need for proactive monitoring and early interventions.

Health.headline: The Best Strength Training Plan Might Be Simpler Than You Think
Health.detail: New strength training guidelines emphasize that doing any resistance training is what truly matters, and experts say even simple routines can increase muscle, strength, and physical function.

Health.headline: Scientists Discover Tiny Rocket Engines Inside Malaria Parasites
Health.detail: Malaria parasites contain tiny spinning crystals powered by a rocket-like reaction that breaks down hydrogen peroxide, releasing energy, which may help the parasite move.

Health.headline: Movement to Phase-Out Mercury in Dental Fillings Notches a Win
Health.detail: The global movement to phase out mercury in dental fillings has gained momentum, marking a significant step towards safer dental practices.

Health.headline: Libraries are becoming hubs for public health across the U.S.
Health.detail: Libraries across the U.S. are increasingly serving as public health hubs, providing access to health information, resources, and programs to address community health needs.

Sports.headline: Maguire and Mainoo recalled in 35-man England squad
Sports.detail: Gareth Southgate has named a 35-man squad for the upcoming international friendlies, with Harry Maguire and Kobbie Mainoo returning to the fold.

Sports.headline: Missouri Tigers vs. Miami Hurricanes: Listen to the Live Radio Broadcast
Sports.detail: Catch every second of the March Madness® First Round® matchup on your radio and the SiriusXM app as the No. 10 Missouri Tigers face off against the No. 7 Miami Hurricanes.

Sports.headline: Colorado Avalanche @ Chicago Blackhawks Game Preview
Sports.detail: The Avalanche begins a four-game road trip with a clash against the Chicago Blackhawks on Friday, marking the third and final regular-season meeting between the teams.

Sports.headline: Storm suffer recurring Broncos nightmare
Sports.detail: Craig Bellamy conceded his side needs to get better defensively after they let a 14-0 lead slip to lose to the Broncos 18-14 in an eerily similar defeat to last year's Grand Final.

Sports.headline: Sports Scoreboard – Friday, March 20
Sports.detail: Results from Thursday OHL Brampton 0 North Bay 7 Barrie 4 Peterborough 5 (OT).

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul 'Bachelorette' season cancelled 3 days before premiere
Entertainment.detail: The new season of 'The Bachelorette' starring Taylor Frankie Paul has been cancelled just days before its premiere following allegations of domestic violence.

Entertainment.headline: So long, Jay-Z: Rapper officially changes name to JAŸ-Z
Entertainment.detail: Jay-Z has officially changed his name to JAŸ-Z.

Entertainment.headline: Oscars 2026 Recap: Must-See Moments and What Didn't Make It to TV
Entertainment.detail: A recap of the must-see moments from the Oscars 2026, including moments that didn't make it to TV.

Entertainment.headline: Joseph Duggar, Brother to Josh, Arrested for Alleged Unlawful Sexual Activity With 9-Year-Old Girl
Entertainment.detail: Joseph Duggar, brother to Josh Duggar, has been arrested for alleged unlawful sexual activity with a 9-year-old girl.

Entertainment.headline: Salman Khan's 'Maatrubhumi' delayed after Prashant Tamang death
Entertainment.detail: Salman Khan's 'Maatrubhumi' has been delayed following the death of Prashant Tamang.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ford Motor Company and Borough of Ringwood to Perform Final Cleanup Targeting Groundwater at Ringwood Mines/Landfill Site in New Jersey
Crime & Justice.detail: Ford Motor Company and the Borough of Ringwood will conduct a final cleanup of groundwater at the Ringwood Mines/Landfill Site in New Jersey to address contamination.

Crime & Justice.headline: Tennessee Woman Sentenced to 40 Months in Prison for Conspiring to Create and Distribute Videos Depicting Monkey Torture and Mutilation
Crime & Justice.detail: A Tennessee woman was sentenced to 40 months in prison for her role in conspiring to create and distribute videos depicting the torture and mutilation of monkeys.

Crime & Justice.headline: At least eight killed as Brazilian police carry out lethal favela raid
Crime & Justice.detail: At least eight people were killed during a Brazilian police raid in a favela.

Crime & Justice.headline: Human smuggling investigation leads to jail time for American man caught in Manitoba
Crime & Justice.detail: An American man caught in Manitoba faces jail time as a result of a human smuggling investigation.

Crime & Justice.headline: Vancouver police issue warning after violent offender re-released into community
Crime & Justice.detail: Vancouver police have issued a warning to the public after a violent offender was re-released into the community.

Education.headline: Syria's education minister says wage increase reflects support for teachers
Education.detail: Syria's Education Minister Mohammad Abdul Rahman Turko stated that a new decree raising salaries in the education sector demonstrates the state's recognition of teachers' roles in building society and improving their living conditions is an investment in quality education. The wage increases are part of broader efforts to reform the education system with financial support and professional development programs.

Education.headline: Lawmakers want to quickly get out-of-state teaching licenses qualified for Colorado classrooms
Education.detail: Colorado Senate Bill 126 aims to expedite the process for out-of-state teachers to obtain licenses in Colorado, requiring the Department of Education to grant initial licenses to educators from compact states within 30 days, contingent on a background check. This legislation seeks to address teacher shortages by easing the transition for qualified teachers from other states.

Education.headline: Board of Education honors athletic trainer; top 10 grads revealed
Education.detail: The Grand Island Central School District Board of Education recognized athletic trainer Samara Johnson for her published article in the “Journal of Athletic Training” about a rare lower leg injury in a high school soccer player. The board also honored Grand Island High School's top 10 graduates at the March 9 meeting.

Education.headline: BASSE Charter to close in June
Education.detail: The Bryan Allen Stevenson School of Excellence (BASSE) charter will be revoked at the end of the school year due to concerns about enrollment, finances, and organization, as announced by Secretary of Education Cindy Marten with the State Board of Education's assent. The decision follows a recommendation from the Delaware Department of Education's Charter School Accountability Committee after a formal review.

Education.headline: Cesar Chavez's legacy was a staple in California schools. How will districts move forward?
Education.detail: Following the publication of sexual abuse allegations against Cesar Chavez, California school districts are reevaluating his role in the curriculum and considering removing his name from schools. The Los Angeles Unified School District, while emphasizing the importance of Chavez's advocacy for workers' rights, is also prioritizing support for those who may have been harmed by his alleged actions.

Lifestyle.headline: How to have the best Sunday in L.A., according to Kenny Scharf
Lifestyle.detail: Kenny Scharf shares his recommendations for spending a Sunday in Los Angeles.

Lifestyle.headline: Reheated rivalry: why I'm the champion of leftovers
Lifestyle.detail: Ruby Tandoh explains why reheating leftovers is a great way to enjoy food and reduce waste.

Lifestyle.headline: Flowers, Markets, and Meaning: A New Lifestyle Trend Among China's Youth
Lifestyle.detail: Young people in China are shifting from buying things to chasing experiences such as flower tourism and visiting local markets, which is generating billions in revenue and reviving rural crafts.

Lifestyle.headline: Phuket Sees Increasing Number of Americans Looking to Buy Property for Lifestyle and Investment
Lifestyle.detail: Phuket's transformation into an international residential market is gaining momentum, attracting Americans due to its quality of life, infrastructure, and clear residency pathways.

Lifestyle.headline: Harrogate Neighbours team to cycle 100 miles for Red Nose Day
Lifestyle.detail: The Harrogate Neighbours team is cycling 100 miles for Red Nose Day.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Iran and the new arithmetic of war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Cheap autonomous weapons are overturning the economics of combat, teaching the U.S. important lessons as the cost of defense skyrockets compared to offensive drone capabilities. In the first week of Tehran's retaliation campaign, drones accounted for about 71 percent of recorded strikes on Gulf states.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Medicare-for-all makes a comeback
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Ramesh Ponnuru discusses the resurgence of Medicare-for-all proposals.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Eid under siege: Little to celebrate in Gaza as Israel tightens chokehold
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Al Jazeera reports on the grim Eid celebrations in Gaza as Israel intensifies its control.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Don't restrict our ability to hold public servants accountable
Opinion/Editorial.detail: CT Mirror argues against proposed exemptions to the Freedom of Information Act that would shield public servants' home addresses, hindering accountability. While safety concerns are valid, blanket exemptions impede transparency and critical reporting.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Iran does not need to close the Strait of Hormuz to disrupt it
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Al Jazeera discusses Iran's ability to disrupt the Strait of Hormuz without physically closing it.

World.headline: UK arrests two people accused of trying to enter nuclear submarine base
World.detail: Two individuals have been arrested in the UK for allegedly attempting to gain unauthorized access to a nuclear submarine base, raising security concerns.

World.headline: Madagascar's military ruler decrees that ministers must pass lie detector tests
World.detail: Madagascar's military ruler, Michael Randrianirina, has decreed that all ministers must pass lie detector tests in an effort to root out corruption following the dismissal of the PM and cabinet.

World.headline: Switzerland halts weapons exports to US amid Iran war
World.detail: Switzerland has decided to halt weapons exports to the United States amidst the ongoing war in Iran, signaling a shift in international relations.

World.headline: US considering occupying Kharg Island to force Iran to open Hormuz strait, say reports
World.detail: Reports indicate that the U.S. is considering occupying Kharg Island in an attempt to force Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for oil transportation.

World.headline: Body of missing Illinois student found in waters off beach of Barcelona
World.detail: The body of an American student who went missing in Barcelona has been discovered in the waters off a beach, according to Spanish police.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe launches AMR surveillance in aquaculture
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has taken a decisive step to curb the spread of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) by introducing surveillance in aquaculture. This initiative aims to monitor and control the use of antimicrobials in fish farming to protect both human and animal health.

Zimbabwe.headline: Red Cross first line of defence in disasters – Minister Muchinguri-Kashiri
Zimbabwe.detail: Minister Muchinguri-Kashiri has recognized the Red Cross as the primary responder in disaster situations. This acknowledgment highlights the crucial role the organization plays in providing aid and support to communities affected by various crises.

Zimbabwe.headline: Treasury releases funding to GMB to clear outstanding arrears
Zimbabwe.detail: The Treasury has released funds to the Grain Marketing Board (GMB) to clear outstanding arrears. This move is aimed at ensuring that farmers are paid for their deliveries, promoting confidence in the agricultural sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: Follow the law, embassy tells Chinese firms
Zimbabwe.detail: The Chinese Embassy in Zimbabwe has cautioned its nationals and companies to adhere to local laws, following Zimbabwe's decision to halt exports of raw minerals and lithium concentrates. The embassy emphasized the importance of risk assessment and compliance with Zimbabwean regulations for Chinese investors.

Zimbabwe.headline: Nurses at Sally Mugabe Central Hospital stage a sick-out to demand pay rises
Zimbabwe.detail: Nurses at Sally Mugabe Central Hospital have initiated a sick-out protest, picketing outside the facility to advocate for salary increases. The demonstration highlights the ongoing concerns regarding remuneration and working conditions within the healthcare sector.
